name: Linzer Augen (Linzer Eyes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg until just combined.
2. Finely chop blanched sliced almonds.
3. Combine 12 oz apricot or raspberry jam with 2 oz water in a small saucepan.
4. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until boiling.
5. Strain into another pan and simmer until slightly thickened.
6. Sift confectioners' sugar over the almond-coated dough rounds.
7. Bake the bases for about 15 minutes at 325°F (160°C), keeping them very pale.
8. Remove from oven and cool completely.
9. Fill the openings of half the dough rounds with the warm glaze using a pastry brush.
Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Beat butter until light, then beat in sugar until very light. Stir in almonds and flour. Scrape onto plastic wrap, flatten, chill for about an hour. Roll to 1/4-inch thickness. Cut into rounds with a fluted cutter. Transfer to paper-lined pans, cut a 1-inch opening in half the rounds using a plain cutter. Coat one side of each round with beaten egg and scatter sliced almonds on top. Fill the other halves with glaze and adhere the coated sides together.
